action.hypothesis: After two consecutive K128 loss-path probes (`AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625` and `DEAD_FEATURE_THRESHOLD=100000`) both failed to beat the declared `topk/signum/LR=1.6e-3/K=128/EF=12` anchor, the most useful single question is whether the exact reference recipe itself still reproduces a clean late-phase descent under the current stack. This round makes no recipe change; it reruns the fully explicit mainline baseline to refresh the curve-shape reference before any further LR or optimizer decisions.
action.summary: Run a clean full baseline on the exact active topk reference recipe. Keep `ARCHITECTURE=topk`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=1.6e-3`, `K=128`, and `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12` fixed, and explicitly lock the auxiliary/runtime knobs to their reference values so the next decision is grounded in a fresh comparable curve rather than in two regressed loss-side variants.
action.self_review: This stays on the active family, respects the one-question rule, and follows the stated priority order by re-establishing a clean baseline before more recipe movement. The risk is no frontier gain this round, but with two consecutive non-improving non-core tweaks, re-anchoring the exact reference is the clearest way to avoid compounding noise and to judge later changes by curve shape rather than stale memory.
notes_to_memory: Rounds 23 and 24 both regressed the K128 anchor, so the loss-side branch currently looks unpromising without a fresh control run.
A full explicit rerun of the exact topk/signum/LR=1.6e-3/K128/EF12 recipe is now the cleanest single-question action because it refreshes the reference curve shape under the current execution stack.

Judge this round primarily by reproducibility and late-phase slope: if the clean baseline again descends smoothly into the known FVU band, move next to LR or optimizer decisions with higher confidence; if it drifts materially, treat stack variance as a live factor before drawing more recipe conclusions.
next_hypotheses: If the clean baseline reproduces the anchor cleanly, the next mainline question should return to a core axis, starting with a narrow LR check or an optimizer comparison per policy order.
If the clean baseline comes in materially worse or with a different curve shape, pause further recipe interpretation and treat baseline reproducibility as the immediate issue.
If the clean baseline unexpectedly improves, promote the refreshed explicit recipe as the operative anchor before any further exploration.
